author: tersec
content:
One general concern about the order-dependency: some are not order-dependent in any intrinsic way, but throw off either REJECT or IGNORE detectably. Nimbus intentionally rearranges these sometimes because various gossip validations have performancewise suboptimal ordering for efficiently rejecting messages. But this can change whether one sees REJECT or IGNORE, even though the net effect on the network is comparable (not identical).

The right answer is ultimately to address these in the spec, but in the meantime, we're not going to randomly pessimize our code

author: tersec
content: But also, the "address these in the spec" strarts venturing towards a kind of performance optimization it's not clear the spec should even have

author: terencechain
content:
this is a good spec test candidate, we found out about this bug today. For attestations in block, we missed the following check. 
```
if block_slot == attestation.data.slot:
        assert attestation.data.index == 0
```
that means a bad proposer/attester could propose a block, and pack an attestation such that its block slot == attestation slot, but yet vote for 1, and in our fork choice accounting, we would have chosen full over empty even majority PTC voted for not available 
<@592004585506340885>

author: jtraglia
content: Yup I understand & agree. Let’s deal with this if/when it comes up. For now, the important thing is that the networking specs are more explicit/clear as executable code. Worst case, the existing tests could be thought of as internal unit tests.

author: jtraglia
content: Are we talking about the `validate_on_attestation` function? There should be a reference test for this already. See [`test_validate_on_attestation_later_slot_full_vote_valid`](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/6ebb2216ca8d7fdac7c108fcfddb74d0e56c42ab/tests/core/pyspec/eth_consensus_specs/test/gloas/fork_choice/test_on_attestation.py#L57-L85). This test hits that assert.

content: This was in the alpha.9 release. Could you confirm you're running that test?
